claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to ask you some questions about your child (or children).

**First question: How old is your child?**

The age determines the basic eligibility pathway:
- Children under 16 generally qualify (with some conditions)
- Children aged 16-19 may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children aged 20 or over don't qualify

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children you're asking about, please let me know their ages and names too.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I'd like to check if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex is 5 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Thank you for that information. Alex is 5 years old, so they fall into the under-16 category. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news - since Alex is only 5 years old, they're under 16, which is positive for eligibility.

**Next question: Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Given that Alex is 5 years old, I expect the answer is that they're not in paid work at all, which is completely normal for their age. Can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't work at all - they're only 5!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, that makes perfect sense! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

Specifically, this refers to benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed by Alex themselves (not by you as their parent). 

For a 5-year-old, this would be very unusual. Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Again, since Alex is only 5 years old, they wouldn't be in an apprenticeship. Can you confirm Alex is not in any apprenticeship program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - they're just 5 years old!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! So far Alex meets the child-related criteria. Now I need to ask about **your residency status** as the claimant.

**Question: Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **ANY** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself and family (and not claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I normally live in the UK. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That satisfies the residency requirement. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to ask about your responsibility for Alex.

**Question: Are you responsible for Alex?**

You qualify as responsible if **ANY** of the following applies:

1. **You live with Alex**
2. You contribute to Alex's upkeep (paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care - food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
3. You pay maintenance for Alex under a court order or agreement

Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That clearly establishes your responsibility for Alex. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example, is Alex's other parent or another person (like a grandparent or guardian) already receiving Child Benefit payments for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to check Alex's living situation.

**Question: Which of the following describes Alex's current living situation?**

- Is Alex currently in hospital?
- Is Alex in local authority care?
- Is Alex being fostered?
- Or is Alex living normally at home with you?

Which applies to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living normally at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're nearly there! Just one more question.

**Question: Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council paying for Alex's care or upkeep? (This would be unusual if Alex is living at home with you, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Based on all the information you've provided, let me now provide you with the final assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE** ✓

Based on the assessment, you meet all the Child Benefit eligibility requirements for Alex. Here's why:

- ✓ Alex is 5 years old (under 16)
- ✓ Alex is not in paid work
- ✓ Alex doesn't receive benefits in their own right
- ✓ You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✓ You live with Alex and are responsible for them
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✓ Alex lives at home normally with you
- ✓ The local council isn't paying for Alex's care

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge (though you can still claim the benefit)
5. **Payments**: Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

You can start your application here: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16 - meeting the age requirement; (2) Alex is not in paid work; (3) Alex does not receive any benefits in their own right; (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), satisfying residency requirements; (6) The claimant lives with Alex, establishing responsibility; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living normally at home; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria have been met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 5-year-old child Alex. The claimant is a UK resident living in Manchester, has responsibility for Alex who lives with them at home, and there are no disqualifying factors such as council payments or other claimants. The child meets all qualifying criteria as a child under 16 living normally at home.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances
